message:
test(harness): conformance ext4 group descriptors vs dumpe2fs

New harness ext4_group_desc_kernel_reference parses every block group
descriptor from a freshly mkfs'd image with both ffs-ondisk's
Ext4ImageReader and dumpe2fs from e2fsprogs, then asserts that every
field — block_bitmap, inode_bitmap, inode_table, free_blocks_count,
free_inodes_count, used_dirs_count, INODE_UNINIT/BLOCK_UNINIT/
ITABLE_ZEROED flags, block_bitmap_csum, inode_bitmap_csum, descriptor
checksum — matches the e2fsprogs reference. The descriptor checksum
is also recomputed from the raw bytes via verify_group_desc_checksum
so a regression in the metadata_csum / gdt_csum routines surfaces here.

Corpus is sized to exceed 50 group-descriptor comparisons across
five image variants chosen for coverage breadth: 384 MiB / 1 KiB
blocks (48 groups, journal + flex_bg), 64 MiB / 4 KiB blocks (1 group,
journal + flex_bg), 256 MiB / 4 KiB blocks (2 groups, no journal),
256 MiB / 4 KiB blocks no flex_bg (2 groups), and 16 MiB / 1 KiB
sanity image. Cross-variant invariants enforce coverage of both block
sizes, both descriptor sizes (32-byte and 64-byte), and at least one
metadata_csum image. The dumpe2fs text parser has its own unit tests
(both flex_bg and native location-line forms, multi-flag and zero-flag
group headers, free-count parsing).

The whole test gracefully self-skips when mkfs.ext4 / dumpe2fs are
absent so it doesn't break sandboxed CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
